title: Heist
subtitle: Heist mechanics and con-artist character pieces — the structural rigor of crime done as craft.

sections.name: I. High Priority
sections.desc: Modern stylish heists + character-driven cons. Direct fits.

items.title: Inside Man
items.whyPriority: Spike Lee's most accessible film. Bank heist that's actually about something else. Denzel + Owen + Foster, all at peak form. Clever structure, genuine surprises.
items.pitch: An NYPD hostage negotiator (Denzel) faces off against a bank robber (Owen) whose plan is more elaborate than it appears.

critics.quote: Roger Ebert: four stars. 'A heist film with literally everything you want plus a few things you didn't know you wanted.'

items.title: The Sting
items.whyPriority: Newman + Redford. 1930s Chicago grifters. Won 7 Oscars including Best Picture. The structural model for every con-artist film since.
items.pitch: Two grifters set up an elaborate revenge against a mob boss who killed their friend.

critics.quote: Won 7 Oscars including Best Picture, Best Director, Best Original Screenplay.

items.title: Catch Me If You Can
items.whyPriority: Spielberg's most charming film. DiCaprio as Frank Abagnale Jr., Hanks as the FBI agent chasing him. A cat-and-mouse character piece more than a heist procedural.
items.pitch: Based on the real Abagnale's life as a teenage check forger and impersonator. Light, fast, surprisingly emotional.

critics.quote: Two Oscar nominations. Walken Best Supporting Actor nomination.

items.title: Logan Lucky
items.whyPriority: Soderbergh's working-class Ocean's Eleven. NASCAR vault heist by West Virginia brothers. Daniel Craig in a wild departure performance.
items.pitch: The Logan brothers plan to rob a NASCAR speedway during a Coca-Cola 600 race. Channing Tatum, Adam Driver, Daniel Craig.

critics.quote: Roger Ebert site: four stars. 'A nimble, witty heist comedy.'

items.title: Paper Moon
items.whyPriority: Black-and-white character piece. Ryan O'Neal and his real-life daughter Tatum (who won the Oscar). Depression-era con artists, hilarious and warm.
items.pitch: A Bible-selling grifter takes a recently orphaned girl across Kansas. She turns out to be a better grifter than he is.

critics.quote: Tatum O'Neal won Best Supporting Actress at age 10 — youngest competitive Oscar winner ever.

sections.name: II. Medium Priority
sections.desc: Ocean's-style polish + character cons + a few experiments.

items.title: Ocean's Eleven
items.whyPriority: The reference modern stylish heist. Soderbergh, Clooney, Pitt, Damon, full ensemble. Mechanically elegant. The reason every other heist film has to compete.
items.pitch: Eleven men plan to rob three Las Vegas casinos simultaneously.

critics.quote: Spawned a successful franchise. The original remains the most assured.

items.title: Heat
items.whyPriority: Mann's masterpiece. De Niro vs. Pacino. The bank robbery downtown LA gunfight set the standard for action cinema. Three hours, all of them earned.
items.pitch: A career thief and the LAPD detective hunting him. Procedural detail of professional crime and professional law enforcement.

critics.quote: Routinely cited among the greatest crime films ever made. Christopher Nolan called it a primary influence on The Dark Knight.

items.title: Rififi
items.whyPriority: Foundational French heist. The 30-minute silent jewel-store break-in is one of the great sequences in cinema. Influenced everything from Ocean's to Mission: Impossible.
items.pitch: Aging thief plans one last job. A Parisian jewelry store. Played as documentary.

critics.quote: Won Best Director.

items.title: American Hustle
items.pitch: 1970s Abscam scandal. Bale, Adams, Cooper, Lawrence, Renner. Long con as ensemble character piece.

critics.quote: Tied record with 10 Oscar nominations across all four acting categories.

items.title: Nightcrawler
items.whyPriority: Adjacent to heist but not exactly. Jake Gyllenhaal as a freelance crime journalist who escalates. Pure character study of someone gaming a system.
items.pitch: An LA drifter discovers crime journalism and becomes increasingly extraordinary at it.

critics.quote: Gyllenhaal Best Actor nomination snub widely considered one of the great Oscar omissions.

items.title: The Killing
items.pitch: Early Kubrick (you've seen all of him, but this fits the heist context). Race-track robbery told in fragmented, overlapping timelines. Tarantino studied it.

critics.quote: Kubrick's first film to receive serious critical attention.

items.title: Dog Day Afternoon
items.pitch: Pacino and Cazale rob a Brooklyn bank. Everything goes wrong. Based on a true story. Won Best Original Screenplay.

critics.quote: Six Oscar nominations including Best Picture. Pacino's career-best by some accounts.

items.title: The Italian Job (1969)
items.pitch: Caine. Mini Coopers. Turin gold heist. The original; the 2003 remake is also fine but the original has the swagger.

critics.quote: British cultural touchstone. The cliffhanger ending is famous.

items.title: Bottle Rocket
items.pitch: You've seen a lot of Anderson. This is his debut — three Texan friends plan inept robberies. Worth seeing if you haven't, since it's the smallest and most overlooked.

critics.quote: Commercial failure on release. Now considered one of the great debuts.

items.title: Reservoir Dogs
items.pitch: Heist after heist film — the actual heist is offscreen. Tarantino's debut. One location, six men, one informant. The launchpad.

critics.quote: Sundance 1992. Established Tarantino. Influence on independent crime cinema is immeasurable.

items.title: The Friends of Eddie Coyle
items.pitch: Robert Mitchum as a small-time Boston gunrunner trying to avoid prison by turning informant. Bleak, anti-glamorous, definitive.

critics.quote: Critical reappraisal has elevated it to canonical status. Roger Ebert: 'A masterpiece of pessimism.'

sections.name: IV. International / Open to New
sections.desc: Where I'd push you given your 'open' answer.

items.title: Le Cercle Rouge
items.pitch: Melville's masterpiece. Three criminals, one heist, jazz score, almost no dialogue. The model for stylized minimalist crime cinema.

critics.quote: Routinely cited among the greatest French films of the era.

items.title: A Prophet
items.pitch: Young illiterate Arab man enters French prison. Six years later he runs the place. Crime-as-education.

critics.quote: Grand Prix. Nine César Awards including Best Film.

items.title: Drive
items.pitch: Stunt driver moonlights as getaway driver. Falls for his neighbor. Things spiral. Ryan Gosling, neon-soaked LA, synth score.

critics.quote: Won Best Director.

items.title: Léon: The Professional
items.pitch: Adjacent to heist — a hitman's character study. Reno's career-defining performance. Young Natalie Portman.

critics.quote: Cult French-American crossover; one of the most influential 90s thrillers.

items.title: Headhunters
items.pitch: Norwegian art thief gets in over his head. Tightly plotted, blackly funny.

critics.quote: Norway's highest-grossing film at the time. Tyldum went on to direct The Imitation Game.
